sentiment: We extend a strange tenderness to things built to be destroyed — the monster, the model, the ritual please.

stories.title: That Methyl Methacrylate Tank
stories.summary: A vivid, almost gothic story from the science blog — a tank of methyl methacrylate, the polymer that becomes plexiglass, quietly hardening into permanence. Strange and specific, like most real things.

stories.title: The Melancholy of Slaying Monsters
stories.summary: There is a particular sadness in finishing off a creature that was only doing what it was made to do. The essay wonders what that says about the one doing the slaying.

stories.title: Prompt Politeness Affects LLM Accuracy (2025)
stories.summary: Research confirming that how you ask matters as much as what you ask. The model doesn't care, but somehow the words do — and so does the result.
